mission.title: containerd: failed to adjust OOM score for shim: invalid argument error -- for docker:dind in Kubernetes
mission.description: failed to adjust OOM score for shim: invalid argument error -- for docker:dind in Kubernetes. This issue affects 17+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
**Description**
We're running `docker:dind` in a Kubernetes pod for CI. With the Docker 20 version of dind (which now uses the v2 shim) this is now broken and errors with a message similar to...

steps.title: Apply the fix for failed to adjust OOM score for shim: invalid argument error…
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
docker: Error response from daemon: io.containerd.runc.v2: failed to adjust OOM score for shim: set shim OOM score: write /proc/211/oom_score_adj: invalid argument
```

steps.title: Confirm failed to adjust OOM score for shim: invalid… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/4837

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
